requirements_met: Add TransientIngestError exception class for recoverable failures
Add PoisonEpisodeError exception class for non-recoverable failures
Follow existing error class patterns in fleet_memory.errors
Verify exceptions are not in the same inheritance subtree
Ensure exceptions are distinguishable by type
Document default-to-transient policy in module docstring
validation_results.test_output_summary: 29 passed in 0.05s - All error taxonomy tests passed including inheritance verification, attribute accessibility, type-based catching, and policy documentation checks
validation_results.code_quality: Follows existing fleet_memory.errors patterns; comprehensive test coverage including edge cases for exception subtree independence; module docstring clearly documents default-to-transient policy
edge_cases_covered: PoisonEpisodeError carries both required reason and optional detail
Module docstring explicitly states unenumerated exception policy
Both exceptions can be raised and caught independently

criteria_verification.notes: Both PoisonEpisodeError and TransientIngestError subclass Exception, consistent with existing fleet_memory.errors module. Verified by passing tests: test_poison_episode_error_subclasses_exception and test_transient_ingest_error_subclasses_exception.

PoisonEpisodeError.__init__ accepts required 'reason' parameter stored as self.reason attribute, suitable for DLQ recording. Verified by passing tests: test_poison_episode_error_carries_reason, test_poison_episode_error_carries_optional_detail, and test_poison_episode_error_detail_is_optional.

PoisonEpisodeError and TransientIngestError are separate class definitions with no inheritance relationship, enabling reliable type-based exception handling. Verified by passing tests: test_exceptions_are_not_in_same_subtree, test_poison_episode_error_can_be_raised_and_caught, and test_transient_ingest_error_can_be_raised_and_caught.

Module docstring in errors.py includes Exception Taxonomy section documenting default-to-transient policy: 'Any *unenumerated* exception escaping the service layer is treated as transient (nak + redeliver), never as poison. Losing data is worse than redelivering.' Verified by passing test: test_module_docstring_states_default_to_transient_policy.

test_exceptions_are_not_in_same_subtree verifies: (1) neither exception is a subclass of the other via issubclass(), (2) attempting to catch PoisonEpisodeError as TransientIngestError fails, (3) attempting to catch TransientIngestError as PoisonEpisodeError fails. This ensures no accidental cross-catching. Test passed in independent verification.

rationale: All 5 acceptance criteria independently verified through 29 passing tests. No honesty discrepancies detected (score: 1.0). All quality gates passed. Implementation follows existing patterns, includes comprehensive edge case coverage, and clearly documents the default-to-transient policy. The exception taxonomy is correctly designed with no inheritance relationship between PoisonEpisodeError and TransientIngestError, preventing accidental cross-catching.
